response.justification: Wei Chen is a skilled full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role. C++ appears only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project — neither demonstrates the depth, recency, or systems-level mastery (STL, multi-threading, memory management) the role demands. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 or SDL compliance experience, MQTT or industrial protocol familiarity (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.), or the security-focused mindset required for this position. The candidate's 15+ years are almost entirely in web, mobile, and TypeScript/Node.js ecosystems, which are largely orthogonal to the core competency profile.
strengths: Solid event-driven and distributed-systems experience (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of requests/second, GISCollective platform)
Demonstrated agile engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and peer mentoring across multiple roles
Familiarity with REST APIs, Node.js, and some cloud tooling (AWS CDK) — covers a small subset of the advantageous skills
concerns: C++ experience is superficial and over a decade old; no evidence of STL, concurrent programming, or high-performance systems work in C++
Zero exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, or any of the industrial protocols listed (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No security engineering background (SDL, application security risk management) — a non-negotiable requirement for this role
